orchestrationSubtypes.label: command -> agent -> skill
orchestrationSubtypes.canonical_projection: Workflow entrypoint resolves intent, hands off to a specialist agent, loads supporting skills, and validates the outcome.

orchestrationSubtypes.label: skill -> skill
orchestrationSubtypes.canonical_projection: Composable skill pipeline with a typed handoff contract between sequential skills.

orchestrationSubtypes.label: orchestrator -> specialists -> validator
orchestrationSubtypes.canonical_projection: Control-plane orchestration pattern where an orchestrator delegates to specialists and accepts only validated outputs.

patterns.intent: Expose a root instruction file that Foundry can install and document per runtime.
patterns.canonical_surface: One primary repository instruction file is installed at the runtime's canonical path.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: One primary repository instruction file is installed at the runtime's canonical path.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If the runtime has multiple rule surfaces, Foundry declares one primary instruction surface and documents additional runtime-owned companions.
patterns.projection_rules: If the runtime has multiple rule surfaces, Foundry declares one primary instruction surface and documents additional runtime-owned companions.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform publishes one install target for repository-level instructions.
patterns.docs_contract: Document file name, install path, and whether companion rule files also exist.
patterns.test_contract: Generated docs and install metadata must agree on the primary instruction path.

patterns.intent: Describe how runtime instruction files layer and override one another.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry records load order, precedence, and monorepo behavior for each runtime.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry records load order, precedence, and monorepo behavior for each runtime.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If hierarchy is not fully native, Foundry documents the degraded loading contract and keeps repo rules route-first.
patterns.projection_rules: If hierarchy is not fully native, Foundry documents the degraded loading contract and keeps repo rules route-first.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares precedence and whether descendant or ancestor loading is native or degraded.
patterns.docs_contract: Document load order, precedence, and monorepo notes.
patterns.test_contract: Capability data must contain a support entry for hierarchy on every platform.

patterns.intent: Support a personal override layer when the runtime allows it.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry distinguishes team-shared instructions from personal overrides.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry distinguishes team-shared instructions from personal overrides.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If no dedicated override file exists, Foundry maps the concept to local user-only settings or documents the limitation.
patterns.projection_rules: If no dedicated override file exists, Foundry maps the concept to local user-only settings or documents the limitation.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Every platform declares either a native override surface or a degraded personal-only workaround.
patterns.docs_contract: Document override path or degraded substitute.
patterns.test_contract: Validation fails if a platform omits override semantics entirely.

patterns.intent: Model global, project, and invocation-time configuration layers.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry emits the user config path, project config path, and one-off override surfaces per runtime.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry emits the user config path, project config path, and one-off override surfaces per runtime.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If the runtime lacks a native project config, Foundry maps project policy into generated rules and install metadata.
patterns.projection_rules: If the runtime lacks a native project config, Foundry maps project policy into generated rules and install metadata.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform defines user/project/invocation config behavior.
patterns.docs_contract: Document config paths, precedence, and override surfaces.
patterns.test_contract: Generated config matrix must list all platforms with non-empty entries.

patterns.intent: Represent named safety and execution presets across runtimes.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry defines review, development, unattended, and trusted presets, mapped to native profiles or degraded install-time presets.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry defines review, development, unattended, and trusted presets, mapped to native profiles or degraded install-time presets.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If profiles are not native, Foundry documents equivalent install-time presets and warnings.
patterns.projection_rules: If profiles are not native, Foundry documents equivalent install-time presets and warnings.
patterns.acceptance_contract: All platforms expose review, development, and unattended preset semantics.
patterns.docs_contract: Document whether presets are native profiles or degraded mappings.
patterns.test_contract: Safety docs must include review/dev/unattended rows for every platform.

patterns.intent: Provide a user-facing entrypoint for structured workflows.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry projects workflows into native commands, prompts, or workflow skills depending on runtime.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry projects workflows into native commands, prompts, or workflow skills depending on runtime.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If a runtime lacks a native workflow primitive, Foundry uses the nearest supported entry surface and documents it as degraded.
patterns.projection_rules: If a runtime lacks a native workflow primitive, Foundry uses the nearest supported entry surface and documents it as degraded.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each workflow has one per-platform entry surface and generated artifact.
patterns.docs_contract: Document the entry surface by platform.
patterns.test_contract: Manifest and generated files must agree on each workflow artifact.

patterns.intent: Use reusable skills as supporting context or user-invocable workflows.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry keeps skills canonical in one source tree and projects them into runtime-specific skill surfaces.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry keeps skills canonical in one source tree and projects them into runtime-specific skill surfaces.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If skills are not native, Foundry projects them into routeable knowledge modules or generated workflow surfaces.
patterns.projection_rules: If skills are not native, Foundry projects them into routeable knowledge modules or generated workflow surfaces.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares how canonical skills are surfaced.
patterns.docs_contract: Document native skill path or degraded equivalent.
patterns.test_contract: Validation fails if canonical skills are silently omitted for a platform.

patterns.intent: Describe how skills are discovered and when they are loaded.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry captures skill discovery rules, precedence, and lazy/full loading behavior.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry captures skill discovery rules, precedence, and lazy/full loading behavior.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If the runtime lacks auto-discovery, Foundry uses explicit route resolution and documents the difference.
patterns.projection_rules: If the runtime lacks auto-discovery, Foundry uses explicit route resolution and documents the difference.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares local/user discovery and auto-discovery semantics.
patterns.docs_contract: Document discovery paths and precedence behavior.
patterns.test_contract: Parity docs must list discovery semantics for all platforms.

patterns.intent: Normalize the supported SKILL frontmatter fields across runtimes.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry records the canonical frontmatter model and per-platform support level for each behavior class.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry records the canonical frontmatter model and per-platform support level for each behavior class.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: Unsupported fields are downgraded to documentation-only behavior and surfaced as degraded.
patterns.projection_rules: Unsupported fields are downgraded to documentation-only behavior and surfaced as degraded.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares whether frontmatter is native, sanitized, or degraded.
patterns.docs_contract: Document the supported field surface and sanitization behavior.
patterns.test_contract: Validation fails if a platform with skills lacks a frontmatter support entry.

patterns.intent: Carry argument-substitution semantics such as $ARGUMENTS or positional args.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry treats string substitutions as part of the canonical skill contract and documents their runtime projection.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry treats string substitutions as part of the canonical skill contract and documents their runtime projection.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If a runtime lacks direct substitution support, Foundry maps the values into workflow prompt text or arguments.
patterns.projection_rules: If a runtime lacks direct substitution support, Foundry maps the values into workflow prompt text or arguments.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares how skill arguments are bound.
patterns.docs_contract: Document argument binding semantics and limits.
patterns.test_contract: Capability matrix must include argument binding for every platform.

patterns.intent: Allow a skill to run in isolated execution when the runtime supports it.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry models isolated skill execution as a first-class projection, even when it degrades to agent handoff.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry models isolated skill execution as a first-class projection, even when it degrades to agent handoff.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If the runtime lacks forked skill execution, Foundry maps it to a specialist or subagent handoff.
patterns.projection_rules: If the runtime lacks forked skill execution, Foundry maps it to a specialist or subagent handoff.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares native or degraded isolation for skills.
patterns.docs_contract: Document how isolated execution is achieved per runtime.
patterns.test_contract: Validation fails if isolation-dependent patterns lack a declared projection.

patterns.intent: Register specialist agents in the runtime's native format.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry projects specialist agents into native agent files, agent config, or generated command surfaces.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry projects specialist agents into native agent files, agent config, or generated command surfaces.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If runtime-native agents do not exist, Foundry projects specialist routes into generated commands or prompts.
patterns.projection_rules: If runtime-native agents do not exist, Foundry projects specialist routes into generated commands or prompts.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Every platform declares how specialists are registered.
patterns.docs_contract: Document native agent paths or degraded specialist routes.
patterns.test_contract: Manifest agent entries must match generated agent artifacts.

patterns.intent: Provide narrow, opinionated specialist personas for domain work.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry specialists remain semantically stable even when projected into different runtime surfaces.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry specialists remain semantically stable even when projected into different runtime surfaces.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If a runtime cannot spawn native agents, Foundry still projects specialist routes with explicit scope and verification contracts.
patterns.projection_rules: If a runtime cannot spawn native agents, Foundry still projects specialist routes with explicit scope and verification contracts.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Every platform maps each shared specialist into a native or degraded surface.
patterns.docs_contract: Document specialist routing semantics and scope limits.
patterns.test_contract: Validation fails if a shared specialist has no platform projection.

patterns.intent: Allow agents to preload skills as background knowledge.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry captures preloaded-skill semantics separately from user-invocable skills.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry captures preloaded-skill semantics separately from user-invocable skills.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If preloading is not native, Foundry injects attached skill guidance into the generated specialist surface and marks it degraded.
patterns.projection_rules: If preloading is not native, Foundry injects attached skill guidance into the generated specialist surface and marks it degraded.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares how preloaded skills are attached to agents.
patterns.docs_contract: Document native preload wiring or degraded attached-skill section.
patterns.test_contract: Generated agent projections must include attached skills when declared.

patterns.intent: Fan out work to multiple agents or specialists in parallel.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry treats multi-agent fanout as a control-plane pattern with runtime-specific execution surfaces.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry treats multi-agent fanout as a control-plane pattern with runtime-specific execution surfaces.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If native multi-agent support is absent, Foundry degrades to orchestrator-managed repeated delegation with explicit ownership.
patterns.projection_rules: If native multi-agent support is absent, Foundry degrades to orchestrator-managed repeated delegation with explicit ownership.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares native or degraded parallel delegation support.
patterns.docs_contract: Document concurrency, depth, and fanout notes.
patterns.test_contract: Capability matrix must include multi-agent orchestration for all platforms.

patterns.intent: Run many homogeneous tasks through a worker fanout pattern.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry models structured batch fanout separately from general orchestration.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry models structured batch fanout separately from general orchestration.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If batch fanout is not native, Foundry maps it to orchestrator-managed repeated delegation and marks the limits.
patterns.projection_rules: If batch fanout is not native, Foundry maps it to orchestrator-managed repeated delegation and marks the limits.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares native or degraded batch fanout semantics.
patterns.docs_contract: Document batch size, timeout, and collection constraints.
patterns.test_contract: Validation fails if a platform omits batch fanout semantics entirely.

patterns.intent: Capture the major composition styles used across upstream repos and Foundry.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry normalizes command -> agent -> skill, skill -> skill, and orchestrator -> specialists -> validator as explicit orchestration subtypes.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry normalizes command -> agent -> skill, skill -> skill, and orchestrator -> specialists -> validator as explicit orchestration subtypes.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If a runtime cannot express a subtype natively, Foundry routes it through the nearest control-plane equivalent.
patterns.projection_rules: If a runtime cannot express a subtype natively, Foundry routes it through the nearest control-plane equivalent.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Every platform declares support for each canonical orchestration subtype.
patterns.docs_contract: Document subtype support and runtime projection rules.
patterns.test_contract: Orchestration docs must include all three subtypes.

patterns.intent: Separate implementation from validation and review.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry keeps validation as a first-class stage with specialist or workflow support.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry keeps validation as a first-class stage with specialist or workflow support.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If a runtime lacks a dedicated validator surface, Foundry uses a degraded review workflow contract.
patterns.projection_rules: If a runtime lacks a dedicated validator surface, Foundry uses a degraded review workflow contract.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Every platform defines a review/validation projection.
patterns.docs_contract: Document review and validation entry surfaces.
patterns.test_contract: Validation data must include a review/validation support entry for each platform.

patterns.intent: Escalate from repo evidence to official docs and then community evidence only when needed.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry enforces repo-first and official-first research policy in rules, workflows, and docs.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry enforces repo-first and official-first research policy in rules, workflows, and docs.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: When the runtime lacks tool-level enforcement, Foundry degrades to generated steering and workflow contract language.
patterns.projection_rules: When the runtime lacks tool-level enforcement, Foundry degrades to generated steering and workflow contract language.
patterns.acceptance_contract: All platforms declare research policy enforcement behavior.
patterns.docs_contract: Document repo-first and official-first escalation behavior.
patterns.test_contract: Rules and docs must align on research policy wording.

patterns.intent: Model project memory and specialist memory loading behavior.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry documents main memory files, specialist memory behavior, and foundation-doc loading order.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry documents main memory files, specialist memory behavior, and foundation-doc loading order.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If runtime-native memory is weak or absent, Foundry degrades to project foundation docs and route contracts.
patterns.projection_rules: If runtime-native memory is weak or absent, Foundry degrades to project foundation docs and route contracts.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares native and degraded memory behavior.
patterns.docs_contract: Document root memory files, specialist memory, and load order.
patterns.test_contract: Memory docs must cover all platforms.

patterns.intent: Capture project-scoped versus global-scoped installation behavior.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry records where project and global artifacts land for every platform.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry records where project and global artifacts land for every platform.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If runtime scope is not fully symmetric, Foundry documents the normalized install behavior.
patterns.projection_rules: If runtime scope is not fully symmetric, Foundry documents the normalized install behavior.
patterns.acceptance_contract: All platforms publish project/global scope paths.
patterns.docs_contract: Document scope paths and precedence.
patterns.test_contract: Scope matrix must include all supported platforms.

patterns.intent: Model runtime hook support and degraded rule-based equivalents.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry treats hooks as native where available and advisory rule reinforcement elsewhere.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry treats hooks as native where available and advisory rule reinforcement elsewhere.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If runtime hooks are not native, Foundry projects the pattern into generated steering, prompt guards, or install guidance.
patterns.projection_rules: If runtime hooks are not native, Foundry projects the pattern into generated steering, prompt guards, or install guidance.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares native or degraded hook support.
patterns.docs_contract: Document event support and degraded alternatives.
patterns.test_contract: Validation fails if hook support is omitted from a platform contract.

patterns.intent: Connect external MCP servers through runtime-specific configuration.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry emits shared MCP guidance plus runtime-specific install targets.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry emits shared MCP guidance plus runtime-specific install targets.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If direct MCP support is limited, Foundry routes through its gateway and documents the degraded behavior.
patterns.projection_rules: If direct MCP support is limited, Foundry routes through its gateway and documents the degraded behavior.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Every platform defines an MCP client surface.
patterns.docs_contract: Document config location, scope, and security guidance.
patterns.test_contract: MCP matrix must include all platforms.

patterns.intent: Limit MCP access to the agents or workflows that need it.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry models scoped MCP access as a separate pattern from global MCP wiring.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry models scoped MCP access as a separate pattern from global MCP wiring.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If per-agent scoping is not native, Foundry documents the nearest workflow or install-time narrowing pattern.
patterns.projection_rules: If per-agent scoping is not native, Foundry documents the nearest workflow or install-time narrowing pattern.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares agent- or route-scoped MCP support.
patterns.docs_contract: Document native scoped MCP behavior or degraded equivalent.
patterns.test_contract: Capability matrix must include scoped MCP support for every platform.

patterns.intent: Expose the runtime itself as an MCP server when supported.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry treats runtime-as-server as a first-class capability and documents gateway fallbacks elsewhere.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry treats runtime-as-server as a first-class capability and documents gateway fallbacks elsewhere.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If a runtime cannot act as an MCP server, Foundry documents the gateway or external server substitute.
patterns.projection_rules: If a runtime cannot act as an MCP server, Foundry documents the gateway or external server substitute.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares native or degraded runtime-as-server behavior.
patterns.docs_contract: Document whether the runtime can expose an MCP server surface directly.
patterns.test_contract: Validation fails if a platform omits runtime-as-server semantics entirely.

patterns.intent: Use a shared browser capability for live verification when configured and allowed.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry normalizes browser verification around shared Playwright/browser MCP policy.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry normalizes browser verification around shared Playwright/browser MCP policy.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If browser tooling is unavailable, Foundry documents the constraint and keeps the policy explicit.
patterns.projection_rules: If browser tooling is unavailable, Foundry documents the constraint and keeps the policy explicit.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Every platform declares browser verification support.
patterns.docs_contract: Document browser/MCP safety rules and available surfaces.
patterns.test_contract: Browser matrix must cover all platforms.

patterns.intent: Map runtime sandbox or containment modes into a shared safety model.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry defines review-safe, dev-safe, and trusted execution boundaries for each runtime.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry defines review-safe, dev-safe, and trusted execution boundaries for each runtime.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If sandboxing is not native, Foundry degrades to rule guidance and environment constraints.
patterns.projection_rules: If sandboxing is not native, Foundry degrades to rule guidance and environment constraints.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform defines review/dev/trusted containment behavior.
patterns.docs_contract: Document sandbox modes and practical defaults.
patterns.test_contract: Safety matrix must include sandbox behavior for every platform.

patterns.intent: Map runtime approval or permission modes into shared autonomy presets.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry defines review, development, unattended, and trusted autonomy levels per runtime.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry defines review, development, unattended, and trusted autonomy levels per runtime.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If approval modes are not native, Foundry degrades to documented operator policy and install-time guidance.
patterns.projection_rules: If approval modes are not native, Foundry degrades to documented operator policy and install-time guidance.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares review/dev/unattended approval behavior.
patterns.docs_contract: Document approval modes and dangerous combinations.
patterns.test_contract: Safety docs must cover approval behavior on every platform.

patterns.intent: Resume a previous task context when the runtime supports session persistence.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry models session resume separately from memory and config layering.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry models session resume separately from memory and config layering.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If session resume is not native, Foundry degrades to fresh-session handoff artifacts and explicit stateless continuation.
patterns.projection_rules: If session resume is not native, Foundry degrades to fresh-session handoff artifacts and explicit stateless continuation.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares native or degraded resume behavior.
patterns.docs_contract: Document session resume semantics and limitations.
patterns.test_contract: Session matrix must include resume support for every platform.

patterns.intent: Fork an existing task context to explore alternatives without mutating the original.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry models context forking as a separate workflow primitive.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry models context forking as a separate workflow primitive.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If session forking is not native, Foundry degrades to explicit handoff packs or fresh-session replay.
patterns.projection_rules: If session forking is not native, Foundry degrades to explicit handoff packs or fresh-session replay.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares native or degraded fork behavior.
patterns.docs_contract: Document forking semantics and caveats.
patterns.test_contract: Session docs must cover fork behavior for every platform.

patterns.intent: Run non-interactive execution in CI or scripts when the runtime supports it.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry records headless or print-mode execution support and machine-readable output support.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry records headless or print-mode execution support and machine-readable output support.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If headless execution is not native, Foundry degrades to stateless invocation guidance and marks machine-readable output limits.
patterns.projection_rules: If headless execution is not native, Foundry degrades to stateless invocation guidance and marks machine-readable output limits.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares native or degraded headless support.
patterns.docs_contract: Document headless invocation and structured output support.
patterns.test_contract: Session/headless matrix must include all platforms.

patterns.intent: Allow isolated worktree execution when the runtime supports it.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry keeps worktree isolation as a first-class runtime capability.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry keeps worktree isolation as a first-class runtime capability.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If worktree support is not native, Foundry degrades to documented external git-worktree workflows.
patterns.projection_rules: If worktree support is not native, Foundry degrades to documented external git-worktree workflows.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares native or degraded worktree behavior.
patterns.docs_contract: Document whether worktree isolation is runtime-native or external.
patterns.test_contract: Capability matrix must include worktree behavior for every platform.

patterns.intent: Support recurring or scheduled workflows when available.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry models scheduled work as a runtime capability with degraded automation fallbacks.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry models scheduled work as a runtime capability with degraded automation fallbacks.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If scheduling is not native, Foundry degrades to explicit external automation or inbox-driven recurrence.
patterns.projection_rules: If scheduling is not native, Foundry degrades to explicit external automation or inbox-driven recurrence.
patterns.acceptance_contract: Each platform declares native or degraded scheduling behavior.
patterns.docs_contract: Document native scheduling or degraded automation guidance.
patterns.test_contract: Validation fails if scheduling semantics are omitted from a platform contract.

patterns.intent: Track upstream runtime capability drift against audited reference repos.
patterns.canonical_surface: Foundry emits an audit artifact and generated docs that record audited references, parity impacts, and blockers.
patterns.canonical_user_experience: Foundry emits an audit artifact and generated docs that record audited references, parity impacts, and blockers.
patterns.degraded_projection_policy: If runtime metadata is partial, Foundry still records the audit entry and any projection gaps.
patterns.projection_rules: If runtime metadata is partial, Foundry still records the audit entry and any projection gaps.
patterns.acceptance_contract: The bundle ships audited reference inputs and a generated audit report.
patterns.docs_contract: Document audited refs, parity impacts, and current blockers.
patterns.test_contract: Validation fails if the audit artifact or audited refs are missing.
